danger_signals.visual_form: Overhead tactical map showing enemy unit icons in red/contrasting color
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat threat; player must engage or retreat

danger_signals.visual_form: Severed arm/leg limbs on battlefield, unit sprite missing limbs
danger_signals.meaning: Progressive mechanical failure; loss of weapons or mobility indicates increasing danger

danger_signals.visual_form: Single-body large enemy units distinct from multi-part wanzers
danger_signals.meaning: High-threat boss-class enemies; significantly stronger than standard wanzers

danger_signals.visual_form: Center torso of wanzer removed from play field entirely
danger_signals.meaning: Unit complete loss; mission failure if allied commander's wanzer destroyed

safety_signals.visual_form: Point-and-click world map nodes designated as intermission zones
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone where player can customize units, organize forces, and prepare for next mission

safety_signals.visual_form: Battle zones become visually inaccessible/grayed out after completion
safety_signals.meaning: Threat neutralized; area is no longer a danger zone

safety_signals.visual_form: Friendly wanzer icons on tactical map (implied color distinction from enemies)

faction_indicators.faction: USN (United States of the New Continent)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: USN has dedicated campaign scenario with distinct unit compositions
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Huffman Island as primary operational theater; coastal/volcanic island terrain

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Wanzer designs likely differ from player faction; vehicles and aircraft used as support

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: World map reveals new accessible locations as plot progresses; briefing UI shows enemy force composition
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player transitions from narrative/dialogue scenes to tactical preparation in intermission zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overhead tactical grid view with wanzer unit sprites, weapons fire animations, part destruction effects
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Turn-based tactical combat; units respond to player commands with movement and attack animations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Battle zone becomes inaccessible on world map; UI indicates mission completion
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Story progresses; new locations unlock; player returns to town for unit customization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wanzer limbs visually disappear from sprite; individual part health bars deplete on UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Damaged units lose weapon function or movement capability; performance degrades progressively

environmental_storytelling.example: Island location roughly the size of Oahu, created by volcanic activity, establishes geopolitical significance and resource conflict context
USN scenario variant allows players to experience conflict from different perspective, implying multi-faction political complexity
New world map locations unlock as plot advances, communicating military campaign progression and territorial control shifts
Mission locations become inaccessible after completion, visually marking conquered/secured territory

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Full movement, weapon use, evasion capabilities; can engage in melee and ranged attacks
Loss of weapon functionality; reduced combat effectiveness; continued movement possible
Movement reduced to single space per turn; evasion severely compromised; immobile tactical threat
Unit removed from play completely; permanent loss; mission failure if player commander's unit
Single-body structure; significantly higher damage output than standard wanzers; boss-tier threat level
Engages player in non-campaign battles; responds to tactical player positioning; fights to destruction

notes: Front Mission communicates world-state primarily through overhead tactical map presentation and progressive location unlock mechanics. Danger is conveyed through enemy unit presence, destructible part systems creating visible degradation, and single-body boss-class enemies. Faction identity is implied through campaign variants (USN scenario) rather than explicit visual sigils in available source material. The modular wanzer damage system creates emergent danger communication—destroyed limbs are immediately visible and mechanically restrict unit capabilities, creating intuitive danger escalation without requiring text warnings.
